column1: # ZAP Alert Rule Overrides for BATbern
# Format: alertId
# Actions: IGNORE | WARN | FAIL
# Omitting url/parameter means the rule applies to ALL URLs.
# Run `zap-api-scan.py --help` for full option list.
# Alert IDs: https://www.zaproxy.org/docs/alerts/
# Base64 Disclosure: JWT tokens in Authorization headers look like "base64"
# Timestamp Disclosure (Unix epoch): timestamps in JSON API responses are intentional
# Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments: React build artifacts & Javadoc strings
# Storable and Cacheable Content: API cache (Caffeine 15min TTL) is by design
# Retrieved from Cache: expected when ZAP requests cached resources
# Sub Resource Integrity Attribute Missing: assets loaded from CloudFront CDN
# Modern Web Application: React SPA detection, not a vulnerability
# Private IP Disclosure: internal AWS VPC IPs that may appear in error traces
# ─── INFORMATIONAL — WARN (non-blocking, report only) ─────────────────────────
# Cookie No HttpOnly Flag: Cognito auth cookies managed by AWS SDK, not us
# Cookie Without Secure Flag: same — Cognito SDK sets these
# Content-Security-Policy Header Not Set: set by Spring SecurityHeadersFilter,
# may not be visible to ZAP through CloudFront
# Permissions Policy Header Not Set: set by Spring SecurityHeadersFilter
# User Agent Fuzzer: not a vulnerability, just header variation probing
# ─── FAIL (block CI) — high-confidence critical findings ─────────────────────
# Nothing explicitly set to FAIL here — let ZAP's default severity thresholds apply.
# Tighten this after the first clean run on staging.
